 {"id":"bd-3pp3","title":"Task: Integrate checkpoint loading for recovery","description":"## Purpose
Load the most recent checkpoint for a session to restore working state.

## Scope
- Load checkpoint.LoadLatest(sessionName) for prior state
- Extract working directory, git branch, file positions
- Include checkpoint timestamp for staleness assessment
- Handle missing/corrupted checkpoints gracefully

## Checkpoint Data Used
- WorkingDir: Last directory agent was in
- GitState: Branch, uncommitted changes
- ActiveFiles: Files being edited
- LastPrompt: What user was asking

## Acceptance Criteria
- Latest checkpoint loaded when available
- Graceful degradation if no checkpoint exists
- Corrupted checkpoints logged but don't block spawn
- Checkpoint age shown in recovery summary

## Technical Notes
Existing checkpoint code in internal/checkpoint/
May need to add LoadLatest() if not exists","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:34:23.660336039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:27:04.355457298Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:27:04.355388298Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: GetLatest() in storage.go:246, loadRecoveryCheckpoint() in spawn.go:1248 integrates checkpoint loading with age limit checking and recovery context building","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pp3","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3pqjq","title":"Task: Add SLB tool adapter (detect/version/health)","description":"## Overview
Implement a tools adapter for Simultaneous Launch Button (slb).

## Responsibilities
- Detect binary (`slb`)
- Parse version output
- Capabilities: approvals, robot_mode (if available)
- Health check: `slb status` or minimal safe check

## Acceptance Criteria
- Adapter reports installed + version correctly
- Health check returns clear message when slb missing","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

-{"id":"bd-3q3u","title":"E2E Tests: Multi-agent communication flow","description":"E2E Tests for Multi-agent communication flow.

## Test Scenarios

1. Basic send/receive
   - Spawn 2 agents
   - Agent 1 sends message to Agent 2
   - Verify Agent 2 receives
   - Agent 2 replies
   - Verify thread maintained

2. Broadcast messaging
   - Spawn 3 agents
   - Send broadcast message
   - Verify all agents receive
   - Verify no duplicates

3. Urgent message handling
   - Spawn agent
   - Send urgent message
   - Verify immediate alert
   - Verify agent acknowledges

4. ntm mail command
   - Run ntm mail myproject cc_1
   - Verify inbox displayed
   - Test send, reply subcommands

## Detailed Logging
```
[E2E-COMM] Sending: from=FROM to=TO subject=SUBJECT
[E2E-COMM] Delivered to N recipients in DURATION
[E2E-COMM] Inbox checked: N new messages
[E2E-COMM] Urgent alert shown to: AGENT
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Basic send/receive preserves thread context
3. Broadcast reaches all agents without duplicates
4. Urgent messages trigger immediate alert
5. ntm mail command displays inbox correctly
6. Detailed logging with [E2E-COMM] prefix
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify multi-agent communication
2. Agent mail delivery tested
3. Thread creation and replies work
4. File reservations respected
5. Test cleanup removes test messages
6. Test duration under 5 minutes","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:14:32.284520831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T05:12:26.587981165Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3q3u","depends_on_id":"bd-1s6h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q3u","depends_on_id":"bd-3hrk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3q483","title":"Task: Implement partial synthesis recovery","description":"## Implementation
Add checkpoint + recovery mechanics to the ensemble manager:

1. Checkpoint storage:
   - Write ModeOutput to `.ntm/ensemble-checkpoints/<run-id>/` after each mode
   - Include metadata: timestamp, status, context_hash

2. Recovery commands:
   - `ntm ensemble resume <run-id>`
   - `ntm ensemble rerun-mode <run-id> <mode>`

3. Cleanup:
   - Remove checkpoints after success
   - `ntm ensemble clean-checkpoints` for manual cleanup

## Logging
- Log checkpoint writes + resume/rerun actions

## Tests
- Unit: checkpoint save/restore
- Integration: resume skips completed modes
- E2E: failure recovery scenario + cleanup

## Dependencies
- EnsembleManager (bd-lp5h4)
- State persistence (bd-25wul)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:19:30.227070060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T18:06:54.831381319Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T18:06:54.831361542Z","close_reason":"Implemented partial synthesis recovery:

1. Checkpoint storage (internal/ensemble/checkpoint.go):
   - CheckpointStore: save/load ModeCheckpoint to .ntm/ensemble-checkpoints/<run-id>/
   - CheckpointMetadata: tracks session, question, completed/pending/error mode IDs
   - ModeCheckpoint: stores output, status, tokens, context hash per mode
   - CheckpointManager: coordinates checkpoint operations during runs

2. Recovery commands (internal/cli/ensemble.go):
   - ntm ensemble resume <run-id>: resume interrupted runs
   - ntm ensemble rerun-mode <run-id> <mode>: re-run specific mode
   - ntm ensemble clean-checkpoints: cleanup with --max-age or --all

3. Tests (internal/ensemble/checkpoint_test.go):
   - 16 unit tests covering save/load, metadata, list, delete, status updates
   - Tests for CheckpointManager initialize/record/resume functionality","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3q483","depends_on_id":"bd-25wul","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q483","depends_on_id":"bd-3didt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q483","depends_on_id":"bd-lp5h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3q59o","title":"AgentLauncher: Execute cc/cod/gmi commands via tmux send-keys","description":"## Purpose
Generate the correct launch command for each agent type (cc, cod, gmi) with proper arguments, environment setup, and working directory configuration.

## Implementation Details

```go
// LaunchCommandBuilder generates agent launch commands
type LaunchCommandBuilder struct {
    agentPaths map[string]string // agent_type -> binary path
    envVars    map[string]map[string]string // agent_type -> env vars
    logger     *slog.Logger
}

// Default agent commands
var defaultAgentCommands = map[string]string{
    "cc":  "claude",          // Claude Code CLI
    "cod": "codex",           // OpenAI Codex CLI  
    "gmi": "gemini",          // Google Gemini CLI
}

// LaunchCommand represents a complete agent launch specification
type LaunchCommand struct {
    Binary     string
    Args       []string
    Env        []string
    WorkDir    string
    AgentType  string
}

// BuildLaunchCommand creates the launch command for an agent
func (b *LaunchCommandBuilder) BuildLaunchCommand(spec PaneSpec, workDir string) LaunchCommand {
    binary := b.agentPaths[spec.AgentType]
    if binary == "" {
        binary = defaultAgentCommands[spec.AgentType]
    }
    
    // Build arguments based on agent type
    var args []string
    switch spec.AgentType {
    case "cc":
        args = []string{"--dangerously-skip-permissions"}
    case "cod":
        args = []string{"--quiet", "--auto-approve"}
    case "gmi":
        args = []string{"--non-interactive"}
    }
    
    // Build environment variables
    env := os.Environ()
    if agentEnv, ok := b.envVars[spec.AgentType]; ok {
        for k, v := range agentEnv {
            env = append(env, fmt.Sprintf("%s=%s", k, v))
        }
    }
    
    cmd := LaunchCommand{
        Binary:    binary,
        Args:      args,
        Env:       env,
        WorkDir:   workDir,
        AgentType: spec.AgentType,
    }
    
    b.logger.Info("built launch command",
        "agent_type", spec.AgentType,
        "binary", binary,
        "args", args,
        "work_dir", workDir)
    
    return cmd
}

// ToShellCommand converts to a shell command string for tmux
func (lc LaunchCommand) ToShellCommand() string {
    parts := append([]string{lc.Binary}, lc.Args...)
    return strings.Join(parts, " ")
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log binary path and arguments for each launch
- Log working directory for debugging
- Log any custom environment variables (names only, not values for security)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correct binary name per agent type
- [ ] Appropriate default arguments per agent
- [ ] Custom path override support
- [ ] Environment variable injection
- [ ] Working directory configuration
- [ ] Shell command string generation for tmux
- [ ] Unit tests for each agent type command","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:00:21.108489967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:34:45.869815726Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:34:45.869647419Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3q59o","depends_on_id":"bd-2sn0a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q59o","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3q7l","title":"E2E Tests: Template-based session spawning","description":"## End-to-End Test Suite for Session Templates

### E2E Test Scenarios
1. **Built-in template usage**
   - Run ntm spawn myproject --template=code-review
   - Verify correct agent count
   - Verify personas assigned
   - Verify initial prompts sent

2. **Custom template**
   - Create ~/.config/ntm/templates/custom.yaml
   - Run ntm spawn --template=custom
   - Verify custom config applied

3. **Template listing**
   - Run ntm template list
   - Verify built-in templates shown
   - Verify custom templates shown
   - Verify descriptions accurate

4. **Template validation errors**
   - Create invalid template
   - Attempt to use
   - Verify clear error message
   - Verify suggestions provided

### Detailed Logging
log.Printf("[E2E-TEMPLATE] Loading template: %s", name)
log.Printf("[E2E-TEMPLATE] Config: agents=%v, personas=%v", agents, personas)
log.Printf("[E2E-TEMPLATE] Spawning with template config")
log.Printf("[E2E-TEMPLATE] Sent initial prompt to %s", agent)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:16:58.062002048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:53:46.315544492Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:53:46.315461316Z","close_reason":"Added E2E tests for session templates (spawn/list/show) with validation suggestions","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3q7l","depends_on_id":"bd-24j9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3q7l","depends_on_id":"bd-2b35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
